senses.definition: a heroic or noble song or poem; a long, often elevated piece of writing or speech
examples: The epic was delivered in a grand cantilene that stirred the crowd.
Her cantilene bore the rhythm of an ancient bard.

senses.definition: plural of cantilene; heroic or noble songs or poems
examples: The cantilenes from the ancient collection impressed the scholars.
They recited several cantilenes at the festival.

senses.definition: a long projecting beam fixed at only one end, used in architecture or engineering to support a structure
examples: The balcony is supported by a steel cantilever.
They designed the house with a cantilevered deck.

senses.definition: to support or extend (something) on a cantilever
examples: The balcony cantilevers from the building.
The bridge cantilevers over the river.

senses.definition: supported by a cantilever; extending or projecting from a structure with one end fixed
examples: The cantilevered walkway stretched over the gulf.
A cantilevered balcony offered a dramatic view.

senses.definition: the act of supporting or extending something on a cantilever
examples: The bridge is cantilevering further over the river.
They are cantilevering a new wing from the main building.

senses.definition: plural of cantilever; projecting beams or supports
examples: The bridge uses multiple cantilevers for support.
Designers added several cantilevers to the seaside structure.

senses.definition: to chant or intone (a liturgical or melodic line); to chant with musical or prosodic emphasis
examples: The priest cantillates the psalms during the service.
She cantillates the Latin prayers clearly.

senses.definition: having been cantillated; chanted or intoned in a liturgical manner
examples: The cantillated readings gave the ceremony a solemn tone.
Cantillated prayers filled the chapel.

senses.definition: chants or intones (a liturgical or melodic line)
examples: The cantor cantillates the hymns at dawn.
He cantillates the psalms with a fervent voice.

senses.definition: chanting or intoning in a musical, liturgical, or rhythmic manner
examples: Cantillating voices filled the cathedral.
Her cantillating style gave the speech a ceremonial cadence.

senses.definition: the act of cantillating or the cadence and musical phrasing of a liturgical chant
examples: Cantillation marks guide readers in pronunciation.
The cantillation of the verses added solemnity.

senses.definition: plural of cantillation; instances of cantillating or the cadences of chants
examples: The cantillations of the liturgy echoed through the hall.
They studied cantillations of the ancient texts.

senses.definition: relating to cantillation; having a chant-like or musical cadence
examples: The cantillatory cadence gave the reading a ritual feel.
Its cantillatory rhythm made the poem sound ancient.

senses.definition: in a key or prominent manner; extensively or fully; in a canting or singing way
examples: The pipes cantily echoed through the hall.
They spoke cantily, as if reciting from memory.

senses.definition: a tavern or bar, especially in a Spanish-speaking country or region
examples: We stopped at a cantina for a quick drink.
The cantina near the plaza serves rustic meals.

senses.definition: plural of cantina; taverns or bars
examples: The cantinas along the street offer cheap meals.
We visited several cantinas during our trip.

senses.definition: the quality of being cantankerous, ill-tempered, or quarrelsome
examples: His cantiness made him a difficult colleague.
She was known for her cantiness in the mornings.

senses.definition: plural of cantiness; instances or qualities of cantankerousness
examples: Their cantinesses varied from day to day.
A few cantinesses were evident in his replies.

senses.definition: speaking insincerely or with a sly, affected, or hypocritical tone; also bending or tilting to one side in a way that suggests cant or slope
examples: The canting salesman tried to mislead customers.
The boat was canting to the left after the storm.

senses.definition: in a canting, sly, or hypocritical way
examples: She spoke cantingly, trying to win sympathy.
He smiled cantingly and walked away.

senses.definition: the quality of canting; the state of being canting or sly
examples: His cantingness was off-putting to colleagues.
The cantingness of the argument weakened its trustworthiness.

senses.definition: plural of canting; acts or expressions of canting behavior
examples: They noticed several cantings during the debate.
Her cantings were brushed off as mere humor.

senses.definition: a waiter or sommelier in a cantina or small restaurant; historically, a keeper of a cantina
examples: The cantinier offered a choice of local wines.
A cantinier served the patrons with a friendly smile.

senses.definition: a small bell or small chest; historically a wine collection or cellar in cantinas
examples: They stored bitters in a cantino.
The cantino of wines was carefully labeled.

senses.definition: a call or proclamation; a formal announcement or sound
examples: The cantion of the crowd signaled the start.
A cantion rose from the stage.
